sglang.title: fix(tool call): Fix tool_index in PythonicDetector and issues with mixed output in non-streaming
sglang.author: CatherineSue
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

PythonicDetector doesn't support tool_index in the streaming mode
When there are mixed outputs (text containing both normal_text and function_call), the detect_and_parse doesn't extract the function call as it simply does regex.match

This PR fixes the above issues.

vllm.title: [Doc]  Convert Sphinx directives ( `{class}`, `{meth}`, `{attr}`, ...) to MkDocs format for better documentation linking
vllm.author: Zerohertz
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
This PR converts Sphinx-style documentation directives (e.g., {class}, {meth}, {attr}, ...) to MkDocs-compatible format using the autorefs plugin. This change improves documentation cross-referencing and ensures proper API documentation linking in the MkDocs-based documentation system.
Examp

vllm.title: [V1][Metrics] Add API for accessing in-memory Prometheus metrics
vllm.author: markmc
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The V0 LLM offline inference API exposes per-request metrics via RequestOutput.RequestMetrics. In V1, so far we have chosen to not track per-request metrics or implement this API.
All recent work implementing EAGLE have been using examples/offline_inference/eagle.py which depends on these metrics to

vllm.title: Disable prefix cache by default for benchmark
vllm.author: cascade812
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
v1 has prefix cache enabled by default to improve performance.
However, this creates misleading latency numbers when running benchmarks without explicitly disabling it.
This PR disables prefix cache in the benchmark script to improve usability for new users.

vllm.title: optimize get_kv_cache_torch_dtype
vllm.author: chunxiaozheng
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
STR_DTYPE_TO_TORCH_DTYPE is defined as follows, which supports types other than ["half", "bfloat16", "float"], and fp8 is also included. Therefore, there is no need to list them here again.
STR_DTYPE_TO_TORCH_DTYPE = {
    "half": torch.half,
    "bfloat16": torch.bfloat16,
    "float": torch.float,

vllm.title: [Core] Automatically cast multi-modal input dtype
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Follow-up to #18732

Automatically cast all floating-point tensors to the model's dtype in MultiModalKwargs.
The dtype casting is done in the same step as device transfer to avoid unnecessary copies

@lgeiger can you benchmark this PR to validate that there's no performance regression for Gemma3?

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Mistral tool calling when content is list
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
FIX #18628
Coauthored with @vvidovic . Similar to the existing patch for assistant message content (converting to string) we must convert tool message content to string + make sure the reasoning_content is not present.
Before this PR, the attached issue and test case would result in a parse error li

vllm.title: [CI/Build] [TPU] Fix TPU CI exit code
vllm.author: CAROLZXYZXY
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Make TPU CI pipeline so that

Tests run sequentially because TPU can only one process at the same time.
If one of the tests fails, the command would exit with non-zero code.

vllm.title: [Neuron] Support quantization on neuron
vllm.author: aws-satyajith
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Support quantization on neuron

Adds fbgemm_fp8 support for neuron.
Allows neuron users to run pre-quantized checkpoints. This includes accepting all act_dtypes as the data type validation is performed in NxDI.

RFC: #15970

vllm bench serve --model meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct --endpoint-type openai-chat --endpoint /v1/chat/completions --dataset-name hf --dataset-path Aeala/ShareGPT_Vicuna_unfiltered

vllm.author: cascade812
vllm.bodyExcerpt: bugfix - Set to False instead of True

vllm.title: [Build] Fixes for CMake install
vllm.author: ProExpertProg
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This fixes the CMake install logic such that using cmake --install directly is supported. Necessary to fix CMake-based user workflows.
It also fixes the error on ROCm if CMake is used outside the venv (common for IDE-run CMake).

vllm.title: [Core] Improve Tensor serialisation
vllm.author: lgeiger
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This improves v1 tensor serialisation by directly relying on torch.frombuffer which removes the need for an temporary numpy array which is a bit easier to read.
Here's a small micro benchmark to verify that this is also faster:
import numpy as np
import torch

from vllm.v1.serial_utils import Msgpac

because we did not use the nomic context extension method.
For nomic-ai/nomic-embed-text-v1, input length greater than 2048 will result nan,
For nomic-embed-text-v2-moe the length is set to 512 by sentence_bert_config.json.
context extension

vllm.title: [Bugfix]: correctly propagate errors message caught at the chat_templating step to the client
vllm.author: gcalmettes
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR ensures the error message from the exception caught during the chat templating step is correctly propagated to the client.
The previous implementation converted the error so it is caught in the entrypoints, however, the error message was not propagated, which resulted in an empty message bei

vllm.title: [V1] fix torch profiling for V1 offline scenarios
vllm.author: divakar-amd
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Currently, the torch profiler doesn't capture anything useful for V1 offline benchmarking scenarios as the gpu_worker is attached to a different process.  This PR:

Refactors offline benchmarking scripts to capture profiler information for both V0 & V1 mode.
Dumps kernels summary table automatically
